ref: Anyone who swears by the altar also swears by everything on it.
text: Nti biaa a ɔde afɔrebokyia ka ntam he ka ɔne deɛ ɔya so nyinaa ntam;
translated: He does not take a bribe and does not use his position to oppress anyone.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: Yei nyinaa siiɛ maa deɛ diyifoɔ he kaeɛ he baam, sɛ,
translated: We are going to eat before you get angry

ref: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
text: na nnipadɔm he sane behyiaa mu a na bennya aduane mpo nni.
translated: If you do not listen to your elders, you will not get good advice.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: W’ahennie mmra. Wopɛ nyɛ hɔ, asaase yi so sɛdeɛ ɔteɛ soro.
translated: The world is in a bad state. You want to be somewhere else, on this earth, as if you are in heaven.

ref: On the way they met a man named Simon who was from Cyrene, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross.
text: Befirii adi ɔɔkɔ he, bɛtoo Kireneni beema bi a bɛfrɛ no Simon; ɔno ne bɛhyɛɛ no maa no soaa Yesu asennua he.
translated: They called the name of the place where he was born, Kireneni, and they named him Simon; he was the one they say was born with the power of Jesus.

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho, ɔbɛma saa akoa he adi n’agyapadeɛ nyinaa so.
translated: If someone does not tell him, he will make the servant carry all his loads.

ref: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: Di w’agya ne wo na nni; na afei, Dɔ wo yɔnko sɛ wo ho.”
translated: Don't be angry with him; and now, love him as yourself.”

ref: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
text: Asuafoɔ he tee yei he, ɔyɛɛ bɛ nwanwa kɛse, na bɛkaa sɛ “Sei ne ɔteɛ deɛ a, hwane ne betumi anya nkwa?”
translated: The chiefs gathered and said to him, "If you are a wise man, who can you give the opportunity to?"

ref: he took the seven loaves of bread and the fish and gave thanks. He then broke them and handed them to his disciples, who passed them around to the crowds.
text: na afei ɔfaa paanoo nson ne nkyenam he; na ɔdaa ase wieeɛ he, obubuu mu mmaa asuafoɔ he, na asuafoɔ he nso de maa nnipadɔm he.
translated: He took a handful of the flour and threw it into the sea; he also poured some into the pot, and the pot boiled over, and the people were fed.

ref: Two blind men were sitting beside the road. And when they heard that Jesus was coming their way, they shouted, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Na mmeema anifirafoɔ mmienu bi tete kwane nkyɛne. Berɛ a bɛtee sɛ Yesu ootwam he, bɛteaam sɛ, “Awurade hu yɛ mmɔbɔ, Dawid Ba!”
translated: And lo, two blind men sitting by the roadside, having heard that Jesus was passing by, cried out, saying, “Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!”
